text.title: hybrid-search-implementation Failure Modes
text.summary: Four observable antipatterns when implementing hybrid search.

antipattern.description: Using the default English BM25 tokeniser on a CJK / Arabic / multilingual corpus.
antipattern.reason: Whitespace tokenisation produces single-character or meaningless tokens for non-whitespace languages, leading to near-zero BM25 scores. Detector: BM25 recall on non-English queries ≪ dense recall. Fix: per-language tokenisers; use Lucene/ICU analyzer or Janome for JP.

antipattern.description: Fitting Pinecone's BM25Encoder on a partial corpus, then ingesting more documents without retraining.
antipattern.reason: IDF weights become stale; recall on the newly ingested portion drops. Detector: A/B old-encoder vs new-encoder on a held-out set shows recall regression. Fix: enforce r3-pinecone-bm25encoder — retrain on full corpus.

antipattern.description: Mixing Qdrant SparseVector(indices=, values=) with Pinecone {"indices": [...], "values": [...]} dict shape.
antipattern.reason: The two SDKs use different Python types/conventions. Detector: TypeError at insert time. Fix: encapsulate per-backend in adapters; never pass raw sparse representations across boundaries.

antipattern.description: Running dense leg and BM25 leg serially when the backend does not offer native hybrid.
antipattern.reason: P99 latency doubles vs parallel execution. Detector: p99 latency = dense_p99 + sparse_p99 instead of max(...). Fix: use asyncio.gather or a thread pool to fire both legs concurrently.
